author: Dr. Frank Mueller (Garrett Regional Radiology)
content:
OUTPATIENT PROCEDURE NOTE — GARRETT REGIONAL MEDICAL CENTER
Date: 3/24/2027 (one of the recurring weekly sessions)
Operator: Dr. Frank Mueller (Diagnostic/Interventional Radiology)

INDICATION: Recurrent symptomatic ascites and right hepatic hydrothorax in cirrhosis. Patient referred by University hepatology for weekly therapeutic drainage close to home (she lives locally; the academic center is ~90 minutes away).

PROCEDURES: Ultrasound-guided right thoracentesis and large-volume paracentesis.

PERI-PROCEDURAL TRANSFUSION (per this facility's standing protocol): Because of the patient's elevated INR (2.0), she received 4 units of fresh frozen plasma prior to the procedures, along with 8 g albumin per liter of fluid removed. This is the facility's routine correction for an elevated INR before drainage procedures.

FLUID REMOVED: ~1.2 L pleural (right); ~5.5 L peritoneal. Both straw-colored/serous.

COMPLICATIONS: None. No procedural bleeding. Patient tolerated the session and was discharged home.

PLAN: Continue weekly thoracentesis/paracentesis here for symptom control, with the same peri-procedural FFP + albumin protocol, until the academic team advises otherwise.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Frank Mueller, MD (Garrett Regional Radiology). 3/24/2027. (Representative of the recurring weekly outpatient sessions — see the Procedures tab for the full series, each documenting the same FFP protocol.)
